title: Promotion-Adjusted Net Revenue by Zone and Vehicle Class

landmines.name: promotion-join-fanout
landmines.kills: Joining trips to the M:N trip_promotions and summing (fare - discount) fans out fare_amount: a multi-promo trip contributes its fare once per promo row, inflating net_revenue for every group that contains multi-promo trips. Fare must be aggregated at trip grain (promotions collapsed first).

landmines.name: duplicate-promo-over-discount
landmines.kills: A (trip_id, promo_id) pair can be logged twice (double application that only discounted the rider once). Summing raw trip_promotions rows subtracts that discount twice; dedup to one row per (trip_id, promo_id) before summing.

landmines.name: null-in-not-in-reachable
landmines.kills: The reachability filter written as `pickup_zone_id NOT IN (SELECT dropoff_zone_id FROM trips)` collapses to zero rows because dropoff_zone_id is NULL on non-completed rows; the guard `IN (... WHERE dropoff_zone_id IS NOT NULL)` is required.

landmines.name: correlated-subquery-tle
landmines.kills: Per-trip correlated subqueries for the discount total and penetration produce the right numbers on small inputs but rescan trip_promotions once per completed trip, blowing the time limit at 3M trips while the set-based reference stays well under it.

notes: Black: grain control is the crux. Reference collapses trip_promotions to trip grain (dedup by (trip_id,promo_id), then sum per trip) before joining, keeping every join into trips 1:1. Fully portable (CTEs, LEFT JOIN, COALESCE, SUM(CASE), 1.0* division, NOT IN/IN); mssql-compatible as written. Note the sample already contains stacked same-(trip_id,promo_id) applications (trips 78, 111, 177), so the dedup matters even on the visible fixture. Self-verified on SQLite: reference correct; naive 1 (fan-out) diverges on net_revenue for multi-promo groups; naive 2 (correlated) is TLE at 3M and also WA on the sample (3 rows differ, over-discounts the doubled pairs); naive 3 (unguarded NOT IN) returns zero rows.
